com.tonic.jaloc.impl.queues

Class PDoubleQueue

  • java.lang.Object
    • com.tonic.jaloc.memory.abs.AbstractNativeCollection<A,W>
      • com.tonic.jaloc.memory.abs.AbstractNativeRing<A,W>
        • com.tonic.jaloc.memory.abs.AbstractPrimitiveQueue<PDoubleArray,PDoubleWriter>
          • com.tonic.jaloc.impl.queues.PDoubleQueue
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    java.lang.AutoCloseable

    public final class PDoubleQueue
extends AbstractPrimitiveQueue<PDoubleArray,PDoubleWriter>
    A growable native double FIFO queue on a ring.

    • Constructor Summary

      Constructors 
      Constructor and Description
      PDoubleQueue()
      Creates an empty queue with zero capacity on the system allocator.
      PDoubleQueue(long initialCapacity)
      Creates an empty queue with the given starting capacity on the system allocator.
      PDoubleQueue(NativeAllocator allocator, long initialCapacity)
      Creates an empty queue with the given starting capacity on the given allocator.

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Concrete Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      double dequeue()
      Removes and returns the head element.
      void enqueue(double value)
      Enqueues value at the tail, growing if needed.
      void enqueueAll(double... values)
      Enqueues values left to right in one capacity reservation.
      void ensureCapacity(long requiredCapacity)
      Grows capacity to at least requiredCapacity.
      double peek()
      Reads the head element without removing it.
      void trimToSize()
      Shrinks capacity to the current size.

      • Methods inherited from class com.tonic.jaloc.memory.abs.AbstractNativeRing

        clear

      • Methods inherited from class com.tonic.jaloc.memory.abs.AbstractNativeCollection

        capacity, close, isEmpty, isOpen, size

      • Methods inherited from class java.lang.Object

        equals, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait

    • Constructor Detail

      • PDoubleQueue

        public PDoubleQueue()
        Creates an empty queue with zero capacity on the system allocator.

      • PDoubleQueue

        public PDoubleQueue(long initialCapacity)
        Creates an empty queue with the given starting capacity on the system allocator.
        Parameters:
        initialCapacity - the starting capacity
        Throws:
        java.lang.IllegalArgumentException - if initialCapacity is negative

      • PDoubleQueue

        public PDoubleQueue(NativeAllocator allocator,
                    long initialCapacity)
        Creates an empty queue with the given starting capacity on the given allocator.
        Parameters:
        allocator - the allocator to source memory from
        initialCapacity - the starting capacity
        Throws:
        java.lang.IllegalArgumentException - if initialCapacity is negative

    • Method Detail

      • ensureCapacity

        public void ensureCapacity(long requiredCapacity)
        Grows capacity to at least requiredCapacity.
        Parameters:
        requiredCapacity - the minimum capacity
        Throws:
        java.lang.IllegalArgumentException - if requiredCapacity is negative
        java.lang.IllegalStateException - if closed

      • trimToSize

        public void trimToSize()
        Shrinks capacity to the current size.
        Throws:
        java.lang.IllegalStateException - if closed

      • enqueue

        public void enqueue(double value)
        Enqueues value at the tail, growing if needed.
        Parameters:
        value - the value to enqueue
        Throws:
        java.lang.IllegalStateException - if closed

      • enqueueAll

        public void enqueueAll(double... values)
        Enqueues values left to right in one capacity reservation.
        Parameters:
        values - the values to enqueue
        Throws:
        java.lang.NullPointerException - if values is null
        java.lang.IllegalStateException - if closed

      • dequeue

        public double dequeue()
        Removes and returns the head element.
        Returns:
        the removed element
        Throws:
        java.util.NoSuchElementException - if empty
        java.lang.IllegalStateException - if closed

      • peek

        public double peek()
        Reads the head element without removing it.
        Returns:
        the head element
        Throws:
        java.util.NoSuchElementException - if empty
        java.lang.IllegalStateException - if closed
